mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of co-expansion quarters during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases or 5-day forward returns show no predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 65% of high-inflation months or the signal loses predictive accuracy during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of occurrences or fails to beat a baseline model in walk-forward validation tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal renewed confidence in long-term economic growth and construction sector activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts lose predictive power during credit-constrained environments or forward returns are negative in more than 60% of rising-starts months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates severe supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, which forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The threshold signal fails to predict negative returns during recessionary periods or shows a Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling test windows.
